practiceAreas.intro: Whether you're writing your first Will, protecting assets for your children, or administering a loved one's estate, our specialist solicitors will guide you through the options available to you with clarity and compassion.

cards.title: Wills
cards.description: A properly drafted will is the cornerstone of any estate plan. We help you express your wishes with absolute clarity — protecting loved ones, guarding against disputes, and ensuring your intentions stand the test of time.

cards.title: Trusts
cards.description: Trusts offer a flexible vehicle for protecting assets, supporting vulnerable beneficiaries, and managing inheritance tax efficiently. We design and administer trusts tailored to your family's circumstances and objectives.

cards.title: Probate
cards.description: Administering an estate is a profound responsibility, often at a difficult time. Our probate specialists guide executors and families through every stage with patience, precision, and quiet efficiency.

cards.title: Power of Attorney
cards.description: A Lasting Power of Attorney allows your to choose who speaks for you, while you still have the ability to select the person or persons that you trust to carry out your wishes, for your wellbeing and your estate.

whyItMatters.intro: Estate planning is rarely urgent until it is. Each year, thousands of families face avoidable difficulty because of an outdated will, no will at all, or a missing trust arrangement. Here is what proper planning can deliver.

boxes.title: Protect Your Family
boxes.text: Ensure your loved ones, particularly children and dependents, are provided for exactly as you intend, with named guardians and clear bequests.

boxes.title: Reduce Inheritance Tax
boxes.text: Strategic use of trusts, gifts, and reliefs can substantially reduce the IHT burden on your estate — keeping more of what you've built within your family.

boxes.title: Avoid Lengthy Delays
boxes.text: Dying with an ambiguous will can prolong probate by months or years. Clear documentation means a faster, smoother process for those left behind.

boxes.title: Express Personal Wishes
boxes.text: A will is more than a document, it carries your voice. Specific gifts, charitable donations, funeral preferences, all set down precisely to your wishes.

boxes.title: Provide for Blended Families
boxes.text: Second marriages, step-children, and complex family structures require thoughtful provision. Trusts ensure fairness across generations and households.

boxes.title: Lasting Peace of Mind
boxes.text: Above all, knowing your affairs are properly arranged lets you live each day unburdened, and gives your family a final, quiet gift of certainty.

whyUs.headingBefore: Legal services handled with clarity and

columns.title: Fixed fees, no surprises
columns.text: We offer clear fixed-fee options, agreed at the outset. You'll always know what the service costs (with no hidden charges) or percentage-of-service billing.

columns.title: A dedicated, expert legal team
columns.text: Experienced solicitors deal directly with the courts, HMRC, Registry Offices, asset-holders, and you have one named contact throughout.

columns.title: Sensitive & supportive firm
columns.text: We understand whether you're dealing with loss or planning for the future. We move at your pace, explain everything plainly, and treat you and your family with warmth and respect.

faq.intro: Few people enjoy thinking about wills and estates, but most questions are simpler than they appear. Below are the queries we field most often,  and if yours isn't here, simply get in touch via our form.

formCard.title: Still have questions or want to start your journey with us?
formCard.subtitle: Our client team are happy to answer anything, with no obligation to proceed.

items.question: How much does it cost to make a will or trust with Devonshires Claims?
items.answer:
Our fees are fixed and quoted in writing before any work begins. A straightforward single will starts at £xxx plus VAT, and mirror wills for couples from £xxx plus VAT. More complex arrangements — those involving trusts, business assets, or tax planning — are quoted individually after your initial consultation.
We never charge by the hour for standard will drafting, so you always know where you stand.

items.question: What happens if I die without a will in England or Wales?
When should I consider setting up a trust?
How long does the probate process usually take?
How often should I review my will?
Can I make a will if I have property or assets abroad?
Are your wills legally binding and recognised everywhere?

banner.paragraph: Clear, professional advice from London solicitors who make writing your will straightforward, so your wishes are followed and your family is spared uncertainty.

overview.headingAfter: service covers and how we support you through it.
paragraphs: A will is the single most important document for protecting your family's future. We make sure yours is valid, tax-aware and genuinely reflects your wishes, written in plain English, not legal jargon.

Whether you need a simple will, a pair of mirror wills with your partner, or something more complex involving property, a business or beneficiaries abroad, our solicitors guide you through every decision and handle the drafting end to end.
We also review and update existing wills after life changes such as marriage, divorce, a new child or buying property, and store your signed will securely, free of charge.
checklist: Free secure storage of your signed will

reasons.headingAccent: is the last thing you want
reasons.subtext: Without a valid will, the law decides who inherits, and it rarely matches what you would have wanted.

cards.title: You decide who inherits
cards.text: Choose exactly who receives your home, savings and possessions, rather than the rigid intestacy rules deciding for you.

cards.title: Protect your children
cards.text: Name legal guardians for children under 18 and set out how and when they receive what you leave them.

cards.title: Prevent family disputes
cards.text: A clear, professionally drafted will removes ambiguity, sparing loved ones stress, cost and conflict later.

cards.title: Reduce inheritance tax
cards.text: Use available allowances and reliefs so more of your estate passes to your family, not to HMRC.

whyUs.headingBefore: Wills expertly crafted for you by

columns.title: Fixed, transparent fees
columns.text: You get a clear, fixed price before any work begins. No hourly billing, no surprises, just one agreed fee for your will.

columns.title: Specialist team of solicitors
columns.text: Your will is drafted by a qualified solicitor, many of our team are full members of STEP, the global body for estate-planning practitioners.

columns.title: Sensitive & supportive firm
columns.text: We understand creating a will can be an emotional and personal experience. We move at your pace, explain everything plainly, and treat you with warmth and respect.

journey.subtext: From your first call to the signing of your documents, our process is designed to be measured, transparent, and respectful of the trust you place in us. For example, most clients complete their will in two appointments.
journey.checklistTitle: What to have ready and consider:
checklist: Photo ID and proof of address
A rough list of your assets & liabilities
Any existing will or trust documents

steps.title: Initial Enquiry
steps.text: A friendly, no-obligation telephone or video call. We listen to what matters to you, outline what may be involved, and provide a clear written quote — no jargon, no surprises.

steps.title: Fact-Find Consultation
steps.text: Meeting in person or remotely, your solicitor takes detailed instructions: family circumstances, asset position, wishes for executors and guardians, and any concerns specific to your situation.

steps.title: Draft & Review
steps.text: We prepare a first draft of your will or trust deed and send it for your review. You will have time to read it carefully, share with family if you wish, and raise any questions before we proceed.

steps.title: Signing & Witnessing
steps.text: Once the document is exactly as you want it, you attend our offices to sign in the presence of qualified witnesses. We handle the formalities precisely as required under the Wills Act 1837.

steps.title: Safe Storage & Aftercare
steps.text: We hold your original will in our fireproof strongroom at no charge for life, register it with the National Will Register, and remind you to review it every five years or after major life events.

items.answer: We work on fixed fees agreed in advance. A single will typically starts from £180 and mirror wills for couples from £300. More complex wills, involving trusts, business assets or overseas property, are quoted individually, always before any work begins.
